name: GPT-4o
memo: OpenAI flagship multimodal model. Excellent vision + text capabilities.

name: GPT-4o Mini
memo: Cost-efficient small model. Replaces GPT-3.5 Turbo.

name: GPT-4.5
memo: Largest and most capable GPT model. Very high cost, best for research and creative tasks requiring broad knowledge.

name: o1
memo: Advanced reasoning model with chain-of-thought. Excels at math, science, and coding.

name: o3-mini
memo: Cost-efficient reasoning model. Strong at math and code with lower latency than o1.

name: o3
memo: Full o3 reasoning model. Top-tier math and science. Replaced o1 as primary reasoning model.

name: o4-mini
memo: Next-gen compact reasoning model with tool use. Strong agentic capabilities.

name: GPT-4.1
memo: 1M context window. Optimized for coding and instruction following. Strong agentic performance.

name: GPT-4.1 Mini
memo: Compact version of GPT-4.1. 1M context at low cost. Good balance for most use cases.

name: GPT-4.1 Nano
memo: Cheapest OpenAI model. Ideal for classification, extraction, and high-volume simple tasks.

name: GPT-5
memo: First model in GPT-5 family. Major leap in reasoning and multimodal. 400K context window.

name: GPT-5.2
memo: GPT-5.2 with 128K output. Enhanced reasoning and coding. Stronger agentic capabilities.

name: GPT-5.4
memo: Latest GPT-5 iteration. State-of-the-art reasoning and coding. Released March 2026.

name: Claude Opus 4.5
memo: Most capable Claude model. Deep reasoning, exceptional coding, strong creative writing.

name: Claude Sonnet 4.5
memo: Best balance of intelligence and speed in Claude family. Ideal for coding and complex tasks.

name: Claude Haiku 3.5
memo: Fast and affordable. Good for classification, simple generation, and high-throughput tasks.

name: Claude Haiku 4.5
memo: Fast and capable. Significant upgrade from Haiku 3.5. Good for latency-sensitive agentic tasks.

name: Claude Opus 4.6
memo: Most capable Claude model. 68.8% ARC-AGI-2. Exceptional coding and deep reasoning. Extended thinking support.

name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
memo: Best coding model in Claude family. 79.6% SWE-bench. Excellent speed-to-quality ratio.

name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
memo: Google flagship with 1M context and built-in thinking. Top benchmark performer with strong multimodal.

name: Gemini 2.0 Flash
memo: Ultra-fast and cheap. Great for high-volume tasks and real-time applications.

name: Gemini 2.5 Flash
memo: Best value thinking model. Built-in reasoning at Flash-tier pricing.

name: Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite
memo: Ultra-low latency and cost. Optimized for high-volume tasks. 1M context window.

name: Gemini 3 Pro
memo: Gemini 3 flagship with 1M context. Major upgrade in reasoning and multimodal. Native tool use.

name: Gemini 3 Flash
memo: Fast and capable. 78% SWE-bench at Flash-tier pricing. 1M context with built-in thinking.

name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
memo: Google latest flagship. 77.1% ARC-AGI-2. Best multimodal model available. Deep thinking with 1M context.

name: Gemma 3 27B
memo: Google open-weight multimodal model. 128K context, 140+ languages. Good for on-prem deployment.

name: Gemma 3 12B
memo: Compact multimodal model from Google. Good balance of size and capability.

name: Mistral Large
memo: Mistral flagship. Strong multilingual capabilities with efficient inference.

name: Mistral Small
memo: Cost-efficient 24B model. Also open-weight under Apache 2.0. Good for latency-sensitive tasks.

name: Mistral Medium 3
memo: Frontier-class multimodal. Strong coding and STEM at competitive pricing.

name: Mistral Large 3
memo: Mistral flagship upgrade. Improved reasoning and multimodal. Strong European language support.

name: DeepSeek V3
memo: 671B MoE with 37B active. Extremely cost-efficient via DeepSeek API. Strong at code and Chinese.

name: DeepSeek R1
memo: Reasoning model built on V3 architecture. Chain-of-thought with MIT license. Rivals o1 at fraction of cost.

name: DeepSeek R1-0528
memo: Major R1 upgrade. Enhanced reasoning with more compute. MIT license. 685B MoE, 37B active.

name: DeepSeek V3.2
memo: Reasoning-first V3.2 with MIT license. Unified reasoning and chat. Near-frontier at fraction of cost.

name: Qwen 3 235B-A22B
memo: 235B MoE with 22B active. Apache 2.0 license. 119 languages. Thinking/non-thinking hybrid modes.

name: Qwen 3 32B
memo: Dense 32B model. Strong reasoning for its size. Thinking/non-thinking hybrid.

name: Qwen 3 8B
memo: Compact 8B dense model. Excellent for edge deployment. Thinking mode available.

name: Qwen 3 30B-A3B
memo: 30B MoE with only 3B active. Extremely efficient inference. Runs fast on consumer GPUs.

name: Qwen 3.5 397B-A17B
memo: 397B MoE with 512 experts, 17B active. Apache 2.0. 201 languages. Rivals Claude Sonnet 4.5 performance.

name: Grok 3
memo: xAI flagship model. Strong at reasoning and real-time data via X integration.

name: Grok 3 Mini
memo: Lightweight reasoning model. Very cost-effective for its capability level.

name: Grok 4
memo: Major upgrade over Grok 3. Strong reasoning with real-time data integration via X.

name: Grok 4 Fast
memo: 40% fewer thinking tokens than Grok 4. 2M context. Ultra-aggressive pricing.

name: Grok 4.1
memo: #1 LMArena Elo 1483. 2M context window. Aggressive pricing at $0.20/$0.50 per million tokens.

name: Command R+
memo: Enterprise-focused with strong RAG capabilities. Excellent at tool use and multi-step retrieval.

name: Command R
memo: Cost-efficient RAG model. Good for multilingual search and simple generation.

name: Command A
memo: 111B enterprise agent model. 150% higher throughput than R+. Strong RAG and tool use.

name: Nova Pro
memo: Amazon Bedrock native model. Multimodal with video understanding. Cost-effective for AWS users.

name: Nova Lite
memo: Ultra-low cost multimodal model. Best for high-volume simple tasks on AWS.

name: Phi-4
memo: 14B model punching above its weight. Excels at math and reasoning for its size. MIT license.

name: Phi-4-mini
memo: 3.8B model with 128K context. Surprisingly capable for its size. Great for mobile/edge.

name: Yi Lightning
memo: Fast MoE-based model with excellent inference speed. Strong Chinese language performance.

name: Kimi K2
memo: 1T MoE with 32B active. Strong coding/agentic capabilities. Open-weight under Modified MIT.

name: Kimi K2.5
memo: 1T MoE with 32B active. Agent Swarm (100 parallel agents). Outperforms GPT-5.2 on vision benchmarks.

name: GLM-5
memo: 744B MoE with 40B active. Apache 2.0 open-source. 200K context. Strong Chinese language model.

name: EXAONE 3.5 32B
memo: Korean-specialized LLM from LG AI Research. Top-tier Korean language understanding and generation.

name: EXAONE 3.5 7.8B
memo: Compact Korean-focused model. Excellent Korean performance for its size.

name: Solar Pro
memo: Korean-optimized model from Upstage. Strong Korean NLP and document understanding.

name: Mistral Small 3.1 24B
memo: Fast and efficient small model with vision capabilities. Best-in-class for its size.

name: Granite 3.1 8B
memo: Enterprise-focused model from IBM. Strong RAG and tool-use capabilities with safety guardrails.

name: Falcon 3 10B
memo: Efficient multilingual model from Technology Innovation Institute. Good general-purpose performance.

name: InternLM 3 8B
memo: Strong tool-use and agentic capabilities. Excellent Chinese and English bilingual performance.

name: Jamba 1.5 Large
memo: Hybrid SSM-Transformer architecture (Mamba + MoE). Extremely long context with efficient inference.

name: OLMo 2 13B
memo: Fully open research model. Training data, code, and weights all publicly available.

name: Nemotron Ultra 253B
memo: NVIDIA flagship reasoning model based on Llama architecture. Optimized for NVIDIA hardware.

name: Qwen 3 0.6B
memo: Ultra-lightweight model for edge/mobile deployment. Minimal resource requirements.

name: Qwen 3 1.7B
memo: Lightweight model for on-device inference. Good balance of size and capability.

name: Qwen 3 4B
memo: Compact model suitable for consumer GPUs. 128K context with good reasoning for its size.

name: Qwen 3 14B
memo: Mid-range model with strong multilingual capabilities. Good for single-GPU deployment.

name: Qwen 2.5 Coder 32B
memo: Code-specialized model. Top-tier performance on SWE-bench and coding benchmarks.

name: QwQ 32B
memo: Reasoning-focused model with chain-of-thought. Competitive with DeepSeek R1 at fraction of the size.

name: GLM-4 9B
memo: Open-source version of GLM-4. Strong Chinese language and tool-use capabilities with 128K context.

name: Qwen 3.5 27B
memo: Hybrid GDN+Attention architecture. SWE-bench 72.4, GPQA 85.5. Native 262K context, 201 languages.

name: Qwen 3.5 35B-A3B
memo: MoE with 8.6% activation ratio. Surpasses Qwen3-235B-A22B. 111 tok/s on RTX 3090 at Q4.

name: Qwen 3.5 122B-A10B
memo: MoE flagship. BFCL-V4 72.2 (tool use) outperforms GPT-5 mini by 30%. AIME 2026 85%.

name: Qwen 3.5 9B
memo: Beats GPT-OSS-120B (13x larger) on GPQA Diamond 81.7. Hybrid GDN+Attention. 262K context.

name: Qwen 3.5 4B
memo: Compact model with 262K context. Hybrid GDN architecture for efficient long-context inference.

name: Qwen 3.5 2B
memo: Lightweight for on-device deployment. 262K context with hybrid attention.

name: Qwen 3.5 0.8B
memo: Ultra-compact for mobile/IoT. Runs video on phones. 262K context.

name: GLM-4.5
memo: Hybrid thinking/non-thinking modes. 3rd place on 12-benchmark aggregate (63.2). Tool-use 90.6% accuracy, surpassing Claude Sonnet 4.

name: GLM-4.5-Air
memo: Efficient variant of GLM-4.5. 106B total / 12B active. Good cost-performance balance.

name: GLM-4.7
memo: Flagship coding/reasoning. SWE-bench 73.8 (OSS SOTA), AIME 95.7, HLE 42.8. Three thinking modes. 200K ctx, 128K output.

name: Kimi-VL A3B
memo: Vision-language MoE. MoonViT encoder, native 1792x1792 resolution. 3B active for fast multimodal reasoning.

name: Kimi-Dev 72B
memo: Code-specialized (Qwen2.5-72B base). SWE-bench Verified 60.4% (OSS SOTA at release). RL-trained on real repos.

name: Kimi Linear 48B-A3B
memo: Delta Attention (KDA) architecture. 1M context, 75% KV cache reduction, 6x decode speedup. Pareto-optimal RULER 128K.
